mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and supports higher equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS 3-month change and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (p>0.05) over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a structural floor, labor market tightness fuels wage inflation, prompting anticipatory Fed tightening that depresses equity prices.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE < 5%, 20-day SPX returns are positive more than 60% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation signal aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, which compresses equity valuations via higher discount rates.
failure_condition: When both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are rising, the 20-day SPX return is positive in more than half of such episodes over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth in a low-inflation environment reflects genuine expansion without overheating, boosting corporate profits and equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In episodes where INDPRO YoY > 3% and CPIAUCSL YoY < 2.5%, 60-day SPX returns are negative or flat with rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts signals collapsing residential investment and consumer confidence, foreshadowing economic weakness that erodes equity valuations.
failure_condition: When HOUST MA(3) < 800, 20-day SPX returns are positive more than 50% of instances or the average return is not significantly negative over a 2-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly gains in payroll employment reflect broad economic strength and rising corporate profitability, pushing equity prices higher in the short term.
failure_condition: The sign of the correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 5-day SPX returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong GDP growth and very low unemployment signal an overheating economy that will eventually force the Fed to tighten, leading to a correction in equity prices over the quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2.5% annualized and UNRATE below 5.0%
failure_condition: In periods with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% and UNRATE < 5%, 60-day SPX returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation above target erodes purchasing power and prompts the Fed to raise rates preemptively; increased discount rates and reduced expected growth compress equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 3% and FEDFUNDS month-over-month increase > 0
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month, 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than half of such episodes or rank IC below 0.03 over 3 years.
